sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Olympique Lyonnais are in exceptional form, riding a 12-match winning streak across all competitions, including a 5-2 thrashing of Metz and a solid 1-0 victory over Lille. Nice, however, have shown inconsistency, managing a 3-2 comeback win against Montpellier but also drawing 2-2 with Brest and losing 1-0 to Ludogorets in their last five matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, results have been split, with Nice winning 3-2 at home in October 2025, while Lyon secured a 2-0 victory away in March 2025. Lyon have historically dominated with 3 wins in the last 5 head-to-heads, suggesting a slight edge in direct matchups.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Lyon, sitting 3rd with 42 points, boast a strong defense (20 goals conceded, 3rd best in the league) and a potent attack led by Pavel Šulc (10 goals). Nice, languishing at 14th with 23 points, have struggled defensively (38 goals conceded) and lack consistent goal-scoring threats, especially with key absences like Mohamed-Ali Cho.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Lyon’s attack will rely on Šulc’s finishing, while Tyler Morton’s recent form (7.52 average rating) adds midfield dynamism; however, they miss Corentin Tolisso. Nice are hampered by multiple absences, including Cho and Hicham Boudaoui, with Sofiane Diop (7 goals) as their main hope.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Lyon’s dominant form, home advantage at Groupama Stadium, and Nice’s defensive frailties, Sofascore’s prediction of a 68% home win probability seems justified. Expect Lyon to control possession (54.9% average) and exploit Nice’s missing players, though Nice might capitalize on counter-attacks as seen in recent games.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Olympique Lyonnais leads Nice with a narrow 1-0 scoreline, thanks to a late goal from Corentin Tolisso in the 45th minute. Lyonnais have shown a slight edge in controlling the game, holding more possession and creating a decent number of entries into the final third. Nice, however, have not been far behind, managing to generate threatening moments despite missing key opportunities. Both teams have shown intent, but execution in front of goal has been the difference so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Lyonnais started strongly with consistent pressure in the opening minutes, maintaining a steady presence in the attacking third. Nice responded with a significant shift in momentum around the middle of the half, putting together several dangerous spells that tested the home defense. As the half progressed, Lyonnais regained control with renewed attacking intent, particularly in the closing stages, culminating in their goal. Nice had brief moments of resurgence, but couldn’t sustain their threat long enough to level the score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers at half-time
halftimeSections.text: For Olympique Lyonnais, Corentin Tolisso has been pivotal, not only scoring the opener but also contributing to the team’s midfield stability. Abner Vinícius has also stood out with creative plays in the final third, posing a constant threat. On the Nice side, players like Mohamed-Ali Cho and Tom Louchet have shown promise in attack, with Cho creating notable opportunities despite missing a big chance. Defensively, Nice will need to tighten up to prevent further breakthroughs from Lyonnais’ attacking players.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Olympique Lyonnais have dominated possession and shown better cohesion in their build-up play, frequently entering the penalty area and winning duels across the pitch. Their ability to win aerial challenges has given them an edge in maintaining control. Nice, on the other hand, have struggled to convert their chances despite creating dangerous situations, with a lack of composure in the final third costing them. They’ve also been less effective in duels, which has allowed Lyonnais to dictate the tempo for large parts of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, Olympique Lyonnais will likely look to build on their lead by maintaining their high possession and pressing Nice in the final third. Nice must improve their finishing if they are to get back into the game, as they’ve shown they can create chances but need to be more clinical. Expect Lyonnais to push for a second goal early on, while Nice will aim to exploit any defensive lapses with quick counter-attacks. The match remains finely poised, and a single moment of brilliance or error could swing the result either way.
